eel: noun. Any freshwater fish of the order Anguilliformes, which are elongated and resemble snakes.
eleven: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 11, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.

elope: verb. Of a married or engaged person, to run away from home with a paramour.

envelop: verb. To surround, enclose or enfold.

novel: adjective. Newly made, formed or evolved; having no precedent; of recent origin; new.

pol: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Polish.

pone: noun. A writ in law used by the superior courts to remove cases from inferior courts.
vee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter V / v.

voe: noun. A narrow sea inlet, particularly in the Shetland Islands, similar to a fjord.

elne: verb. To strengthen; hearten; comfort; encourage.
elven: noun. Originally, a female elf, a fairy, a nymph; any elf.
evene: verb. To occur; to happen; to come to pass.
evoe: interjection. Alternative form of euoi (“Bacchic cry”).
